symptoms.symptom_name: Acute globus sensation (lump-in-throat) with throat tightness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Globus is the defining subjective symptom; non-specific alone but characteristic in context.
Subjective tightness does not localise a mechanical obstruction and can occur with anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subjective choking / feeling that air is being cut off

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catastrophic interpretation of throat sensation is typical of the anxiety-globus cycle.
Alarming but subjective; not corroborated by objective airway signs in this case.

symptoms.symptom_name: Racing heart, hyperventilation and acute panic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic and hyperventilation drive and amplify the globus sensation.
Tachycardia is non-specific; euthyroid state argues against thyrotoxicosis.
Hypervigilant checking behaviour perpetuates the symptom and points to a functional/anxiety mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Able to speak in full uninterrupted sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Full-sentence phonation requires adequate airflow and an open airway — a strong reassuring feature against significant obstruction.
Absence of stridor argues against critical upper-airway narrowing, though patient self-report is less reliable than clinician auscultation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Able to swallow water when tried

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to swallow liquid argues against a high-grade mechanical obstruction or complete dysphagia.
Globus classically spares true swallowing — food/liquid passes despite the lump sensation.
Normal colour argues against critical hypoxia from airway compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known euthyroid Hashimoto's thyroiditis with stable goitre and positive antithyroid antibodies

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documented euthyroid state makes thyroid storm very unlikely.
A known neck lump provides the focus for health-anxiety-driven hypervigilance.
Absence of skin/mucosal and lower-airway features effectively excludes anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central trachea, smooth non-tender goitre, clear chest on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person examination structurally excluded tracheal deviation, goitre haemorrhage and lower-airway pathology — a sealed clinician finding NOT available over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — BP 140/90, HR 105, RR 20, SpO2 100%, afebrile (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and hypertension fit acute anxiety; a normal SpO2 confirms adequate oxygenation — sealed because measured by the clinician, not patient-obtainable here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal thyroid function tests with positive antithyroid antibodies (recent bloods)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical euthyroidism excludes thyrotoxicosis as the driver.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Full-sentence speech, absent stridor, preserved swallowing and normal colour together strongly argue against a significant mechanical airway obstruction and permit telehealth de-escalation.
Sudden globus with panic, hyperventilation and compulsive neck-checking is a coherent functional/anxiety picture over a benign known goitre.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the known goitre and reassures; discussed at GP follow-up, not an acute telehealth order.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat/confirm thyroid function tests via GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms ongoing euthyroid status; routine, not acute.

Not indicated for the current reassuring presentation; would be over-investigation. Reserved for true airway red flags (stridor, rapidly enlarging tender mass, voice change) which would prompt ED assessment.
